name: alex-wmill-alex

metadata.name: alex-wmill-alex
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A client library for accessing Windmill server wrapping the Windmill client API

documentation:
# wmill

The core client for the [Windmill](https://windmill.dev) platform.


## Usage

### Basic Usage

The `wmill` package has several methods at the top-level for the most frequent operations you will need.

The following are some common examples:

```python
import time

import wmill


def main():
    # Get the value of a variable
    wmill.get_variable("u/user/variable_path")
    
    # Run a script synchronously and get the result
    wmill.run_script("f/pathto/script", args={"arg1": "value1"})
    
    # Get the value of a resource
    wmill.get_resource("u/user/resource_path")
    
    # Set the script's state
    wmill.set_state({"ts": time.time()})
    
    # Get the script's state
    wmill.get_state()
```

### Advanced Usage

The `wmill` package also exposes the `Windmill` class, which is the core client for the Windmill platform.

```python
import time

from wmill import Windmill

def main():
    client = Windmill(
        # token=...  <- this is optional. otherwise the client will look for the WM_TOKEN env var
    )

    # Get the current version of the client
    client.version

    # Get the current user
    client.user
    
    # Convenience get and post methods exist for https://app.windmill.dev/openapi.html#/
    # these are thin wrappers around the httpx library's get and post methods
    # list worker groups
    client.get("/configs/list_worker_groups")
    # create a group
    client.post(
        f"/w/{client.workspace}/groups/create",
        json={
            "name": "my-group",
            "summary": "my group summary",
        }
    )
    
    # Get and set the state of the script
    now = time.time()
    client.state = {"ts": now}
    assert client.state == {"ts": now}
    
    # Run a job asynchronously
    job_id = client.run_script_async(path="path/to/script")
    # Get its status
    client.get_job_status(job_id)
    # Get its result
    client.get_result(job_id)


```

functions.name: wmill.client.get_id_token
documentation: Get a JWT token for the given audience for OIDC purposes to login into third parties like AWS, Vault, GCP, etc.

functions.name: wmill.client.duckdb_connection_settings
documentation:
Convenient helpers that takes an S3 resource as input and returns the settings necessary to
initiate an S3 connection from DuckDB

functions.name: wmill.client.polars_connection_settings
documentation:
Convenient helpers that takes an S3 resource as input and returns the settings necessary to
initiate an S3 connection from Polars

functions.name: wmill.client.boto3_connection_settings
documentation:
Convenient helpers that takes an S3 resource as input and returns the settings necessary to
initiate an S3 connection using boto3

functions.name: wmill.client.load_s3_file
documentation: Load the entire content of a file stored in S3 as bytes

functions.name: wmill.client.load_s3_file_reader
documentation: Load the content of a file stored in S3

functions.name: wmill.client.write_s3_file
documentation:
Upload a file to S3

Content type will be automatically guessed from path extension if left empty

See MDN for content_disposition: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Disposition
and content_type: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Type

functions.name: wmill.client.username_to_email
documentation:
Get email from workspace username
This method is particularly useful for apps that require the email address of the viewer.
Indeed, in the viewer context WM_USERNAME is set to the username of the viewer but WM_EMAIL is set to the email of the creator of the app.

methods.name: wmill.client.Windmill.load_s3_file
documentation:
Load a file from the workspace s3 bucket and returns its content as bytes.

'''python
from wmill import S3Object

s3_obj = S3Object(s3="/path/to/my_file.txt")
my_obj_content = client.load_s3_file(s3_obj)
file_content = my_obj_content.decode("utf-8")
'''

methods.name: wmill.client.Windmill.load_s3_file_reader
documentation:
Load a file from the workspace s3 bucket and returns the bytes stream.

'''python
from wmill import S3Object

s3_obj = S3Object(s3="/path/to/my_file.txt")
with wmill.load_s3_file(s3object, s3_resource_path) as file_reader:
    print(file_reader.read())
'''

methods.name: wmill.client.Windmill.write_s3_file
documentation:
Write a file to the workspace S3 bucket

'''python
from wmill import S3Object

s3_obj = S3Object(s3="/path/to/my_file.txt")

# for an in memory bytes array:
file_content = b'Hello Windmill!'
client.write_s3_file(s3_obj, file_content)

# for a file:
with open("my_file.txt", "rb") as my_file:
    client.write_s3_file(s3_obj, my_file)
'''

\:param channel: Slack channel
\:param message: Message to send to Slack
\:param approver: Approver name

methods.name: wmill.client.Windmill.username_to_email
documentation:
Get email from workspace username
This method is particularly useful for apps that require the email address of the viewer.
Indeed, in the viewer context WM_USERNAME is set to the username of the viewer but WM_EMAIL is set to the email of the creator of the app.
